name: Skeleton
description: Animated bones. No will, no personality, just whatever command set them walking.

sensory.sight: the wrong kind of movement — too smooth, too steady, the joints don't work the way living joints work
sensory.sound: the click of bone on stone, rhythmic and relentless, like a broken clock
sensory.feel: cold air, and the wrongness of something that should be still but isn't

encounter_hooks: a burial ground with disturbed earth and empty graves
scratching sounds from behind a sealed tomb door
a single skeletal hand protruding from the ground, fingers twitching
notes: Skeletons are creepy but mechanical. They follow orders until destroyed. The real question is: who gave the order? In Threadhallow, skeletons might be weave-animated — the Tapestry replaying a death, not necromancy.
